title: Nikkei Rises 1.4%, Closing Above 60000 for First Time
body: LIVE Last Updated: 2 hours ago By Kosaku Narioka, Dow Jones Newswires Japan's Nikkei Stock Average climbed 1.4% to 60537.36, closing above the 60000 threshold for the first time. Electronics stocks led the gains, with both Fanuc and Keyence jumping 16% following their quarterly results. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield rose 3 basis points to 2.465%. The dollar was at 159.38 yen, little changed from Y159.37 late Friday in New York.

title: World shares are mostly higher and oil gains $2.50 as Iran talks remain in flux
body: Oops, something went wrong World shares mostly gained and the price of Brent crude oil jumped $2.50 a barrel early Monday as talks on ending the war with Iran stayed snagged. Tokyo's Nikkei 225 index hit a fresh record Monday after U.S. stocks ended last week with new highs. Two Middle Eastern officials said Iran was offering to end its chokehold over the Strait of Hormuz without addressing its nuclear program, while requesting that the U.S. end its blockade of Iran. President Donald Trump is unlikely to support the proposal, which was conveyed to the U.S. through Pakistan, as he wants Iran’s nuclear program closed down as part of a broader deal to end the war.
